$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `conversation_annotations` repo's
`upsert` method (the deferred custom method, after the create/update/delete
round-trip in conversation-annotations-tier2.json). One committed file drives
BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-conversation-annotations-upsert-fixture.ts +
conversation-annotations-upsert-tier2.ts) and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness conversation_annotations upsert tier-2 test).
What this banks: v4's `upsert(input)` — find ONE existing row by the unique key
(chatId, messageIndex, characterName); if found -> `_update(id, {content,
sourceMessageId})` (ONLY those two fields, base `_update` re-mints updatedAt,
preserves id+createdAt); else -> `_create(input)` (mints id + createdAt +
updatedAt = the same now). Because upsert MINTS its own id + timestamps, this
is the minted-values (REMAP) tier-2 form: nothing is pinned on the upsert ops,
and the harness normalizes only the `id` column (first-seen tokens) and the
timestamps before diffing. SEED rows pin ids + timestamps (deterministic
pre-existing state).
Both upsert paths are exercised: op[0] matches seed row 1 -> UPDATE; op[1] is a
new key -> CREATE; op[2] matches the row op[1] created -> UPDATE of a minted
row (its minted id is preserved); op[3] is another fresh CREATE. Every row's
`content` is DISTINCT (the natural-key sort column) and the nullable
`sourceMessageId` is banked both null and non-null.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the other
Phase-2 fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.

seed.content: AAA seed opening flourish, soon to be revised by upsert.
BBB seed later remark, left untouched by the upsert ops.

ops.comment: MATCHES seed row 1's unique key (chatId, messageIndex 0, Prospero) -> UPDATE path. Revises content and sets a non-null sourceMessageId (seed had null). id + createdAt preserved; updatedAt re-minted.

input.content: CCC upsert update of the seed row, now anchored to a source.

ops.comment: NEW unique key (different chat) -> CREATE path. sourceMessageId null. Mints id + createdAt + updatedAt to the same now.

input.content: DDD upsert create on a fresh key, no source message yet.

ops.comment: MATCHES the row op[1] just created (same chat, messageIndex 3, Aurora) -> UPDATE path on a MINTED row: its minted id is preserved, content + sourceMessageId change, updatedAt re-minted. Banks update-of-created idempotency.

input.content: EEE upsert update of the just-created row, now sourced.

ops.comment: Another NEW unique key -> CREATE path with a non-null sourceMessageId.

input.content: FFF upsert create anchored to message eight.
